notes: I expected 'careerism is bad' moralising and got something sharper. The verse explicitly does NOT condemn the demo-week / promo-cycle work: 'the cycle works: promo lands, headline number moves, all-hands applauds. The bhoga-aiśvarya-gati is real.' What it names is the constitutive shift: 'each iteration slightly reshaped what they consider real engineering work.' That is a real diagnostic — I have watched senior engineers' definition of 'real engineering' narrow to whatever fits the slide. The Datadog-query-then-vs-now framing (production curiosity yielding to slide-source query) is recognisable. Falsifiability handles 'organisational legibility is not bad' correctly. Counter-example carves out runway-critical moment. PASS.
